It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also! == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} * It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未}}. For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|12=draft}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|12=draft}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|12=draft}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|12=draft}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|12=draft}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft}} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]][[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]][[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]][[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]]

For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]][[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]][[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]][[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]]

For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]][[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]][[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]][[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]]

For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft}} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]][[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]][[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]][[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} [[Category:pages to sort]][[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]][[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]][[Category:pages to sort]][[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]][[Category:pages to sort]][[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

(Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages to sort]] revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] |qq=}} [[de:Yang Metal Horse]] [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yang Metal Horse|qq=}}

However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yang Metal Horse|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:庚午]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[de:Yang Metal Horse]] [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] }} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[de:Yang Metal Horse]] [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] }} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[de:Yang Metal Horse]] [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-mi|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto-hitsuji|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stem|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branch|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Destructive cycle|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] }} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-mi|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto-hitsuji|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stem|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|金|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branch|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Destructive cycle|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] }} Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|金|" to "|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|"
revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-mi|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|1=Yin Metal Goat|2=ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto-hitsuji|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stem|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branch|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|火|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Destructive cycle|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} * {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly Branches|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branches|qq=}} [[zh-min-nan:Keng-chá]] [[zh-yue:庚午]] {{translated page|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|version=97212183|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] }} [[cdo:Káng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Metall-Pferdevi]] [[de:Yang Metal Horse]] [[sn:Yang Metal Horse]] [[ja:庚午]] [[en:Yang Metal Horse]] [[zh:庚午]] [[ko:경오]] [[fr:Cheval de métal]] [[de:Metall-Pferd]] [[sn:ang Metal Horse]] [[af:Metaalperd (astrologie)]] [[de:Metall-Pferdeen]] [[en:Metal Horse]] [[fr:Cheval de métalboishak]] [[it:Kang-chṳ́id]] [[sn:engwaja]] [[vi:Canh Ngọ]] [[za:Gengceij]] [[ar:Yang Metal Horse]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|火|" to "|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|"

However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other Uses == In {{ill|Hiroshima|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kōgo district|ja|庚午 (広島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo district|qq=}} was named after the year 1870, in which it was developed. == See Also == * Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} * {{ill|Earthly {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Metal Horse|kanji=庚午|romaji=kanoe uma|onyomi=kōgo|p=gēngwǔ|w=keng-wu|mi=kɤŋ⁵³ u²¹|y=geng1 ng5|j=geng1 ng5|ci=kɪŋ ŋoː²|mc=kæŋ ŋoX|oc-bs=kˤraŋ ŋˤaʔ|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Sexagenary cycle|広島市の地名|庚午 (広島市)|北海道の「庚午三ノ村」|円山町 (北海道)|qq=}} {{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Metal Horse also known as Kanoe-uma''' (庚午, also known as '''Kōkin-no-uma''' or '''Kōgo''') is one of the combinations of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|ja|Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}. It is the 7th combination in the sexagenary cycle, following {{ill|Tsuchinoe-mi|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-mi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoe-mi|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} and preceding {{ill|Kanoto-hitsuji|ja|1=Yin Metal Goat|2=ja|辛未|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto-hitsuji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoto-hitsuji|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Metal Goat|qq=}}. In {{ill|Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja|陰陽五行思想|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, the {{ill|Heavenly Stem|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stem|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stem|qq=}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) represents Yang {{ill|Metal|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|qq=}}, while the {{ill|Earthly Branch|ja|十二支|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branch|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earthly Branch|qq=}} '''Uma''' (午) represents Yang {{ill|Fire|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|1=Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|lt=Fire (火)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire|13=User:Immanuelle/Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|qq=}}, resulting in a {{ill|Destructive cycle|ja|五行思想#相剋（そうこく）|相剋|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Destructive cycle|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Destructive cycle|qq=}} (Fire overcomes Metal). == Kanoe-uma Years == For years in the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 10.
For years before the Common Era, a year is a Kanoe-uma year if, when divided by 60, the remainder is 51. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Kanoe-uma Years''' |- ![[1st Millennium BC]]!![[1st Millennium AD]]!![[2nd Millennium AD]]!![[3rd Millennium AD]] |- | *[[951 BC]] *[[891 BC]] *[[831 BC]] *[[771 BC]] *[[711 BC]] *[[651 BC]] *[[591 BC]] *[[531 BC]] *[[471 BC]] *[[411 BC]] *[[351 BC]] *[[291 BC]] *[[231 BC]] *[[171 BC]] *[[111 BC]] *[[51 BC]] | *[[10 AD]] *[[70 AD]] *[[130 AD]] *[[190 AD]] *[[250 AD]] *[[310 AD]] *[[370 AD]] *[[430 AD]] *[[490 AD]] *[[550 AD]] *[[610 AD]] *[[670 AD]] *[[730 AD]] *[[790 AD]] *[[850 AD]] *[[910 AD]] *[[970 AD]] | *[[1030 AD]] *[[1090 AD]] *[[1150 AD]] *[[1210 AD]] *[[1270 AD]] *[[1330 AD]] *[[1390 AD]] *[[1450 AD]] *[[1510 AD]] *[[1570 AD]] *[[1630 AD]] *[[1690 AD]] *[[1750 AD]] *[[1810 AD]] *[[1870 AD]] *[[1930 AD]] *[[1990 AD]] | *[[2050 AD]] *[[2110 AD]] *[[2170 AD]] *[[2230 AD]] *[[2290 AD]] *[[2350 AD]] *[[2410 AD]] *[[2470 AD]] *[[2530 AD]] *[[2590 AD]] *[[2650 AD]] *[[2710 AD]] *[[2770 AD]] *[[2830 AD]] *[[2890 AD]] *[[2950 AD]] |} === Notable Events === * Birth year of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu|qq=}}, the legendary first emperor of Japan. * Year 670 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Nenjaku|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Nenjaku|qq=}}, an important early census system in Japan under {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}}. * Year 1870 AD - {{ill|Kōgo Incident|ja|庚午事変|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Incident|qq=}}. == Kanoe-uma Months == In years where the last digit is 4 or 9 (corresponding to the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|ja|十干|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Stems|qq=}} of Kō (甲) or Ki (己)), the fifth month (May) is a Kanoe-uma month. However, the definition may apply to the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} or {{ill|Solar terms|ja|節月|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar terms|qq=}} (from {{ill|Mangzhong|ja|芒種|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mangzhong|qq=}} to the day before {{ill|Xiaoshu|ja|小暑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiaoshu|qq=}}). == Kanoe-uma Days == === Special Calendar Significance === Kanoe-uma days have the following calendar attributes: * {{ill|Hando|ja|犯土|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hando|qq=}} (First day of major land restriction periods) * {{ill|Shinkichi-nichi|ja|神吉日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkichi-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for shrine visits) * {{ill|Daimyō-nichi|ja|大明日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daimyō-nichi|qq=}} (Auspicious day for general affairs) == Other
